name: Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo
role: President of Mexico (Oct 1, 2024–present)
quote: We are the second floor of the Fourth Transformation. Mexico will not be intimidated by tariff threats. We govern for the Mexican people.

name: Lázaro Cárdenas Batel
role: Chief of Staff / Head of the Office of the Presidency
quote: The coordination between the presidency and congress ensures that the transformation agenda moves forward without pause.

name: Rogelio Ramírez de la O
role: Finance Secretary (SHCP); overseeing fiscal consolidation from 5.7% deficit
quote: Mexico's fiscal path is clearly defined: reducing the deficit while protecting social investment and strategic infrastructure.

name: Juan Ramón de la Fuente
role: Foreign Affairs Secretary (SRE); former UNAM rector and UN ambassador
quote: Mexico maintains its position: sovereignty is non-negotiable, but cooperation between equals is always possible.

name: Marcelo Ebrard Casaubon
role: Economy Secretary; architect of Plan México nearshoring strategy
quote: Plan México is our response to global trade uncertainty — we will turn geopolitical tension into industrial opportunity for Mexico.

name: Omar García Harfuch
role: Public Safety Secretary (SSPC); former CDMX security chief, 2020 CJNG assassination attempt survivor
quote: Mexico's homicide rate is at its lowest in nine years. Our strategy targeting cartel leadership is delivering results the data confirms.

name: Rosa Icela Rodríguez
role: Interior Secretary (SEGOB); continuity minister from AMLO cabinet
quote: The interior agenda focuses on consolidating constitutional reforms and supporting the institutions of the Fourth Transformation.

name: Alicia Bárcena
role: Environment Secretary (SEMARNAT); former ECLAC/CEPAL head and climate expert
quote: Mexico's energy sovereignty and climate commitments are compatible — renewable energy at the national scale can serve both goals.

name: Mario Delgado
role: Public Education Secretary (SEP); former Morena party president who managed Sheinbaum's presidential campaign
quote: Education reform must deliver results in classrooms, not just in legislation. The new textbooks and teacher training initiatives are already changing outcomes.

name: Luz Elena González Escobar
role: Energy Secretary; oversees Pemex/CFE restructuring and energy sovereignty agenda
quote: The energy reform ensures Mexico's oil and electricity remain in the hands of the Mexican state, not foreign investors.

name: David Kershenobich
role: Health Secretary; UNAM researcher with 500+ published medical studies
quote: Public health policy must be grounded in the best available scientific evidence. We are rebuilding Mexico's health system on that foundation.

name: Raquel Buenrostro
role: Anticorruption and Good Governance Secretary (SABG); leads the body that absorbed INAI's transparency functions
quote: The new SABG is more effective than the fragmented system it replaced — accountability with clear executive responsibility.

name: Ricardo Monreal Ávila
role: Senate President and key Morena congressional leader; managed judicial reform passage
quote: The Senate has exercised its constitutional mandate to transform the institutions of the republic. History will judge this the right decision.

name: Hugo Aguilar Ortiz
role: Chief Justice of the Supreme Court (SCJN); elected June 2025; Mixtec Indigenous lawyer with AMLO-era ties
quote: For the first time, the Supreme Court has justices who come from the people, not from the elite law firms that served the powerful.

name: Xóchitl Gálvez Ruiz
role: Former PAN senator; 2024 presidential candidate who lost to Sheinbaum with ~28% of votes; leading opposition voice
quote: Sheinbaum won an election but is governing as though she won a revolution. A 60% mandate is not a license to dismantle democracy.

name: Marko Cortés
role: PAN National President; opposition bloc leader; called Sheinbaum's electoral reform 'Ley Maduro'
quote: We will not allow Mexico to become a one-party state. The PAN will defend democratic institutions in congress and in the courts.

name: Dante Delgado Rannauro
role: Leader of Movimiento Ciudadano (MC); third-largest opposition bloc
quote: Mexico needs a real opposition, not a caricature. Movimiento Ciudadano will be the civic alternative that voters are looking for.

name: Donald Trump
role: U.S. President (Jan 20, 2025–present); signed FTO EO, tariff threats, and deportation directives affecting Mexico
quote: Mexico must do more on fentanyl and illegal immigration. Tariffs are a powerful tool and we will not hesitate to use them until Mexico acts.

name: Marco Rubio
role: U.S. Secretary of State; issued cartel FTO designations Feb 2025; delivered 54-point demands to Sheinbaum Sep 2025
quote: The United States will use every available tool — including FTO designations, sanctions, and tariffs — to dismantle the cartels threatening our citizens.

name: Kristi Noem
role: U.S. DHS Secretary; oversees deportation operations and border wall construction (projected completion Jan 2028)
quote: The era of an open border is over. We are removing every person here illegally and building the wall that will stop future crossings.

name: Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada García
role: Co-founder of Sinaloa Cartel; arrested in U.S. in July 2024 allegedly after being lured by the Chapitos faction
quote: I did not surrender. I was lured and kidnapped by Joaquín Guzmán López and handed to U.S. authorities against my will.

name: Joaquín Guzmán López ('El Chapito')
role: Leader of Los Chapitos faction of Sinaloa Cartel; accused of orchestrating El Mayo's US capture; in US custody
quote: No public statement verified from custody.

name: Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes ('El Mencho')
role: CJNG leader; designated FTO by U.S. Feb 2025; CJNG's Teuchitlán forced recruitment camp discovered Mar 2025
quote: No verified public statement available.

name: Alejandro Gertz Manero
role: Attorney General (FGR); classified Rancho Izaguirre as 'recruitment site' not 'extermination camp'
quote: The evidence from Teuchitlán indicates a forced recruitment and training facility. We have acted accordingly with arrests and prosecutions.
